openSUSE Leap 16.0", "product_id": "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.s390x"}, "product_reference": "gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.s390x", "relates_to_product_reference": "openSUSE Leap 16.0"}, {"category": "default_component_of", "full_product_name": {"name": "gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.x86_64 as component of openSUSE Leap 16.0", "product_id": "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.x86_64"}, "product_reference": "gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.x86_64", "relates_to_product_reference": "openSUSE Leap 16.0"}]}, "vulnerabilities": [{"cve": "CVE-2026-41991", "ids": [{"system_name": "SUSE CVE Page", "text": "https://www.suse.com/security/cve/CVE-2026-41991"}], "notes": [{"category": "general", "text": "GNU gzip contains a vulnerability in the gzexe utility related to insecure temporary file handling. When the mktemp utility is not available in the user's PATH, gzexe falls back to constructing a temporary file path based solely on the process ID (PID). This predictable filename is created without exclusive access or existence checks.
A local attacker can pre-create the predicted temporary file path as a symbolic link pointing to an arbitrary file writable by the victim. When gzexe runs, it follows the symlink and overwrites the target file, resulting in a time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) condition that allows arbitrary file overwrite.

This issue has been fixed in the commit 4e6f8b24ab823146ab8776f0b7fe486ab34d4269", "title": "CVE description"}], "product_status": {"recommended": ["openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.aarch64", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.ppc64le", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.s390x", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.x86_64"]}, "references": [{"category": "external", "summary": "CVE-2026-41991", "url": "https://www.suse.com/security/cve/CVE-2026-41991"}, {"category": "external", "summary": "SUSE Bug 1269622 for CVE-2026-41991", "url": "https://bugzilla.suse.com/1269622"}, {"category": "external", "summary": "SUSE Bug 1269623 for CVE-2026-41991", "url": "https://bugzilla.suse.com/1269623"}], "remediations": [{"category": "vendor_fix", "details": "To install this SUSE Security Update use the SUSE recommended installation methods like YaST online_update or "zypper patch".
", "product_ids": ["openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.aarch64", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.ppc64le", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.s390x", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.x86_64"]}], "scores": [{"cvss_v3": {"baseScore": 7.1, "baseSeverity": "HIGH", "vectorString": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H", "version": "3.1"}, "products": ["openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.aarch64", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.ppc64le", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.s390x", "openSUSE Leap 16.0:gzip-1.13-160000.3.1.x86_64"]}], "threats": [{"category": "impact", "date": "2026-07-18T08:12:44Z", "details": "important"}], "title": "CVE-2026-41991"}]}
